project.summary: Nel ASA commercially launched its next-generation pressurized alkaline electrolyser system, a new technology platform designed to simplify hydrogen production projects while significantly improving cost, efficiency, and scalability.
project.scope: Industrialization of the platform at Nel's Herøya facility in Norway, enabling a production capacity of up to 1 GW per year, with a roadmap to scale up to 4 GW annually. The industrialization is supported by a grant of up to EUR 135 million from the EU Innovation Fund, covering up to 60% of eligible industrialization costs.
project.timeline: Commercial launch on May 6, 2026. Industrialization followed a final investment decision announced in December 2025.
project.why_it_matters: The scale-up of hydrogen production capacity and the development of new electrolyser technology will drive demand for various lab supplies, reagents, and scientific equipment for R&D, quality control, and ongoing production processes.

project.summary: Samsung E&A and Nel Hydrogen ASA launched CompassH2-A+, a new 100-megawatt pressurized alkaline electrolysis platform for efficient large-scale green hydrogen production, introduced at the World Hydrogen Summit in Rotterdam.
project.scope: A compact 100 MW pressurized alkaline electrolysis platform utilizing Nel's pressurized alkaline electrolyzer technology integrated into modular, containerized 25 MW stack units. The core production facility occupies about 7,500 square meters and is capable of producing around 40 tonnes of high-purity hydrogen per day.
project.timeline: Officially launched during the World Hydrogen Summit in Rotterdam.
project.why_it_matters: The introduction of a new, compact, and high-capacity electrolysis platform will create demand for specialized lab equipment, reagents for process monitoring, and consumables for the operation and maintenance of the new production infrastructure.

ai_summary: Nel is aggressively launching new, large-scale pressurized alkaline electrolyser systems, including a 100MW platform with Samsung E&A, to simplify and improve green hydrogen production. This significant technological advancement and commercialization effort means increased R&D, process optimization, and quality control needs. Lab supply sales teams should engage to support their evolving requirements for analytical equipment, consumables, and materials testing as they scale up.
